question.text: If a client's officially valid document does not contain an updated address, which of the following can be accepted as proof of address on a temporary basis?

answer.text: Utility bill not more than two months old, property tax receipt, pension payment order, or employer accommodation letter
Any document regardless of date, as long as it shows an address
Only a bank statement not more than one year old
A self-declaration by the client on plain paper

explanation.text:
**The Logic:** Under Rule 9(18) of PML Rules, when the OVD does not have an updated address, the following can be accepted as temporary PoA: (i) utility bill not more than 2 months old, (ii) property/municipal tax receipt, (iii) pension/family pension payment orders, or (iv) employer accommodation allotment letter. However, the client must submit an updated OVD within 3 months.
**The Trap:** The utility bill must be NOT MORE THAN 2 MONTHS OLD. A bank statement is not in this list. Self-declaration alone is not accepted. The 3-month deadline for submitting updated OVD is critical.
**Cross-Exam Context:** In NISM V-A, mutual fund distributors frequently encounter this situation where a client has moved but hasn't updated their OVD. The temporary acceptance provision helps onboard such clients.
**Distributor's Pro-Tip:** When accepting a utility bill as temporary PoA, note the 2-month validity limit and set a reminder for the 3-month deadline to collect the updated OVD. Document the acceptance in the client file with the expected date for updated documents.
